type.description: Object type defining a set of fields. Each field has a type and optional arguments.

input.description: Input types for mutations and queries. Like types but used as arguments.

enum.description: Enumeration type restricting values to a defined set.

interface.description: Abstract type defining fields that implementing types must include.

union.description: Union of multiple object types. Use inline fragments to query specific types.

scalar.description: Custom scalar type beyond built-in ID, String, Int, Float, Boolean.

schema.description: Root schema definition mapping operation types to root types.

directive.description: Metadata attached to types or fields. Built-in: @deprecated, @skip, @include.

query.description: Read operation. Fetches data without side effects.

mutation.description: Write operation. Modifies data on the server.

subscription.description: Real-time operation. Maintains a persistent connection for live updates.

fragment.description: Reusable set of fields. Spread with ...FragmentName.

ID.description: Built-in scalar representing a unique identifier. Serialized as String.

String.description: Built-in scalar for UTF-8 text strings.

Float.description: Built-in scalar for double-precision floating-point numbers.

non_null.description: Non-null modifier. The field will never return null.

list.description: List modifier. The field returns an array of the specified type.
